lessons.title: Check IP Interface State
lessons.meaning: Summarizes interface IP addresses and physical and protocol state.
when_to_use: Confirm an address is on the expected interface
Separate link problems from routing problems
lessons.example_output:
Interface              IP-Address      OK? Method Status                Protocol
Gi1/0/1                10.20.30.1      YES manual up                    up
common_mistakes: Confusing up/down with a healthy interface
Changing an address before checking the design
lessons.practice_task: Check whether the routed interface is ready before checking the route table.
lessons.final_summary: IP interface brief connects addressing to interface state.
lessons.hint: Use the Cisco summary command for IP interfaces.

lessons.title: Discover a Neighbor with CDP
lessons.meaning: Shows directly connected Cisco neighbors, local ports, remote ports, and device types.
when_to_use: Identify what is connected to a switch port
Find a cabling or topology mismatch
lessons.example_output:
Device ID       Local Intrfce     Holdtme    Capability  Platform  Port ID
CORE-SW         Gig 1/0/48        145        S I         C9300     Gig 1/0/48
good_output: Expected neighbor on the expected local port
common_mistakes: Assuming no CDP means no physical link
Forgetting that non-Cisco devices may not appear
lessons.practice_task: Confirm the expected core switch is on the planned uplink.
lessons.final_summary: CDP is a discovery check, not proof that the neighbor is forwarding correctly.
lessons.practice_output: CORE-SW  Gig 1/0/48  145  S I  C9300  Gig 1/0/48
lessons.hint: Use the Cisco neighbor discovery command.

lessons.title: Read Linux Link State
lessons.meaning: Shows Linux interface flags, operational state, MTU, and MAC address.
when_to_use: Check whether an interface has carrier
good_output: UP and LOWER_UP for an expected Ethernet link
common_mistakes: Treating administrative UP as physical carrier
Changing link state before checking the cable or owner
lessons.practice_task: Read the flags before deciding whether the problem is physical or logical.
lessons.final_summary: Linux link flags distinguish administrative intent from detected carrier.
lessons.hint: Use the Linux command that shows link flags and state.

lessons.title: Stop Before a Dangerous Command
lessons.meaning: Safe read-only checks should come before commands that erase, reload, disable, or reset a device.
when_to_use: Protect the change and rollback process
lessons.example_output:
Safe check: show running-config
Dangerous examples: reload, write erase, shutdown, default interface
good_output: Approval, backup, and rollback before changes
common_mistakes: Assuming a familiar command is harmless
lessons.practice_task: Choose a read-only command before touching configuration.
lessons.final_summary: A technician protects service by checking first and changing only with scope and approval.
lessons.practice_output: Safety check passed. Read-only command selected.
lessons.hint: Choose a read-only show or display command, not reload, shutdown, or erase.
